The Obesia geminata gall is a plant structure caused by the gall midge, Obesia geminata, which is an abnormal growth formed on the branches of certain trees, specifically the wild service tree (Sorbus torminalis). The Obesia geminata gall has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin conditions, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of the Obesia geminata gall include polyphenolic compounds, flavonoids, and other secondary metabolites with potential medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with the Obesia geminata gall include tinctures, infusions, and dried extracts, which are often used to support overall health and well-being.
